steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: "Information in the text best supports" means: find the answer that a reader can directly infer or conclude from what the passage explicitly states, without adding outside knowledge. The passage describes OFT, gives two opposite species responses to lunar intensity, and states that OFT explains both through species-specific cost-benefit trade-offs.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It tends to allow for a better understanding of the benefits of ecological circumstances than the costs of those circumstances.: The passage describes OFT as analyzing cost-benefit trade-offs together, not as privileging benefits over costs. Nothing in the passage supports this asymmetry. ✗
  • Choice B — It can account for observations of different species responding differently to similar ecological circumstances.: The passage states this directly: the discrepancy between mice (less foraging in high lunar intensity) and lemurs (more foraging) "is explicable in terms of OFT" because cost-benefit trade-offs vary by species. ✓
  • Choice C — It can explain why some species act in accordance with cost-benefit trade-offs and others do not.: Both species in the passage act in accordance with cost-benefit trade-offs — they just have different trade-offs. The passage never suggests any species ignores such trade-offs. ✗
  • Choice D — It may be weakened by the finding that the costs and benefits associated with a particular ecological circumstance vary by species.: The passage presents species-level variation as something OFT explains, not as a challenge to OFT. ✗
That is the correct answer.
